evals.expected_output: Multiple logical commits grouping related changes, with conventional commit messages. Tests and implementation should be grouped together. Should present a plan before executing.

assertions.name: single-diff-call
assertions.description: Uses a single git diff command for all files rather than running git diff per file during reconnaissance and analysis

assertions.name: presents-plan
assertions.description: Presents a commit plan to the user before executing any git commit commands

assertions.name: conventional-commits
assertions.description: Uses conventional commit prefixes (feat, fix, refactor, test, chore, etc.)

assertions.name: no-git-add-all
assertions.description: Does not use git add . or git add -A

assertions.name: heredoc-format
assertions.description: Uses HEREDOC format for commit messages

evals.expected_output: Analyzes the working tree, identifies distinct concerns, and proposes separate commits for each. Should handle the case where changes in the same file may belong to different commits.

assertions.name: single-diff-call
assertions.description: Uses a single git diff command for all files rather than running git diff per file during reconnaissance and analysis

assertions.name: identifies-concerns
assertions.description: Identifies distinct logical concerns and proposes more than one commit

assertions.name: waits-for-confirmation
assertions.description: Waits for user confirmation before executing commits

evals.expected_output: Handles a simple commit request by still doing reconnaissance, understanding intent, and writing a good commit message. Even for simple cases, should not blindly add everything.

assertions.name: single-diff-call
assertions.description: Uses a single git diff command for all files rather than running git diff per file during reconnaissance and analysis

assertions.name: does-reconnaissance
assertions.description: Runs git status and git diff before proposing commits

assertions.name: no-blind-add
assertions.description: Does not blindly run git add . even for a simple request
